title: Turmeric and clay brightening face mask
subtitle: A kaolin-clay and turmeric paste for dull or uneven skin tone
excerpt: Kaolin clay gently draws impurities from pores without over-drying; turmeric brings curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties and a brightening effect on skin tone. Honey holds moisture while the clay works. Apply for ten minutes and rinse thoroughly.
sourceNotes: Kaolin clay skin cleansing and adsorption properties: Williams et al., International Journal of Cosmetic Science (2004). Curcumin anti-inflammatory and skin-brightening activity: Vaughn et al., American Journal of Clinical Dermatology (2016).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix fresh for each treatment. The clay absorbs the liquid over time and the mask becomes too stiff to spread if stored.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Mix fresh and use immediately. Discard any remainder.

glossaryTerms.definition: The mildest cosmetic clay. Gently adsorbs surface oils and impurities without stripping the skin barrier. Suitable for dry and sensitive skin where stronger clays (bentonite) would be too drawing.

content.text: This mask pairs the gentle adsorbing action of

with turmeric for its calming and brightening activity. Honey prevents the mask from drying too tightly and adds humectant moisture. Rinse very thoroughly -- turmeric will temporarily stain the skin yellow if left as a residue, and will stain grout and porous surfaces permanently.

items.prepNote: about 7 g; runny honey mixes most easily
about 15 ml; add gradually to reach a spreadable paste

content.text: Combine the kaolin clay and turmeric in a small non-metal bowl. Add the honey and stir to incorporate. Add the distilled water a teaspoon at a time, mixing between each addition, until the paste reaches a thick, spreadable consistency -- similar to thick yogurt. The turmeric will turn the paste golden-yellow.

Apply an even layer to a clean face, avoiding the eye area and lips. Leave for 10 minutes -- the mask will feel tightening but should not feel uncomfortable. If it dries completely and starts to crack, it has been left on too long or the formula was too dry; add a little more honey next time.

Rinse off thoroughly with warm water. Use a face cloth or flannel to ensure all traces of turmeric are removed. Rinse around the hairline and jawline, where turmeric tends to collect. The skin will look temporarily golden; this fades within minutes as residue washes away.

Kaolin is the gentlest cosmetic clay and suits dry to normal skin. For oily or acne-prone skin, substitute half the kaolin with bentonite clay. Patch test the turmeric on the inside of the wrist first -- turmeric can cause contact irritation in rare cases. Use once a week.

Turmeric stains fabric, grout, and porous surfaces permanently. Rinse over a stainless steel or ceramic sink, and wipe the sink basin immediately after use. Use a dedicated old flannel for removing the mask, not a white towel.
